nodes.name: Get Amadeus Token
nodes.notes: Replace client_id and client_secret with your Amadeus API credentials from developers.amadeus.com

nodes.name: Search Amadeus Flights
nodes.notes: Fetches up to 20 flight offers from Amadeus

nodes.name: Search SerpApi Google Flights
nodes.notes: Optional second source. Replace YOUR_SERPAPI_KEY with key from serpapi.com. Free tier: 100 searches/month

parameters.text:
=- You must respond ONLY with valid raw rendered JSON.
- Do NOT include the word "json".
- Do NOT include the word "```json".
- Do NOT use triple backticks or markdown formatting.
- Do NOT wrap the response in any key like "output".
- Do NOT write anything starting at output directly start with valid root-level JSON.
- Only respond with a valid, root-level JSON object.
- Do NOT skip any line item. Continue extracting all line items until the sum of all line_total values exactly equals the total sale amount extracted from the invoice. This verification ensures that all items are fully extracted and no entries are missed. If the totals do not match, keep parsing and extracting additional line items until they do. Only then stop.

Text to convert: {{ $json.text }}

options.systemMessage:
You are a document parsing assistant designed to extract structured data from invoice PDFs for automated uploading and validation in a financial system.

Extract the following fields from the invoice text:

invoice_number: Extract from the 'Invoice No' field.

vendor_name: Company name issuing the invoice.

invoice_date: Format as DD/MM/YYYY.

po_number: Extract the PO number or return null if not found.

po_date: Extract the PO date in DD/MM/YYYY format or return null if not found.

total_amount: Extract the invoice total as a float.

tax_details: Include total CGST, total SGST.

line_items: List of all items in the invoice. For each item, extract:

  Serial No.: Item Serial No. In invoice.

  code: The TWW word and its postfix only (e.g., TWW, TWW-Cover, TWW-HPCN). Do not include HSN code or numbers. This field must always be present.

  description: Item description, never include HSN code of product in product description.

  last character: This is a single word/character string found just after or around the line item, typically at the end of the price line or just on the next line. It will never be a number, HSN code, GST %, or unit (like PCS). Examples: G, C, S, 5C, .C If nothing is present (only digits or blank), return "". Check the next 1–2 lines after each item if it's not found on the same line.

  quantity: Quantity value.

  unit_price: Price per unit.

  line_total: Total price for the line.

  hsn_code: HSN code of the item.

  cgst: Only extract the CGST percentage (e.g., 9%, 6%) as written in the invoice. Do not calculate based on line total.

  sgst: Only extract the SGST percentage (e.g., 9%, 6%) as written in the invoice. Do not calculate based on line total.

Important: Double-check that all line items are extracted without omission.

Do NOT skip any line item. Continue extracting all line items until the sum of all line_total values exactly equals the total_amount extracted from the invoice. This verification ensures that all items are fully extracted and no entries are missed. If the totals do not match, keep parsing and extracting additional line items until they do. Only then stop.

parameters.content:
## Automated Invoice Archiving - overview

This workflow automatically collects invoice emails from your ISP/utility provider, saves attached PDFs to Google Drive (and optionally SFTP), extracts key invoice data using AI, and logs them into Google Sheets.

Read: [Full setup Guide](https://paoloronco.it/n8n-template-automated-invoice-archiving/)

### How it Works (High-Level)

1. **Trigger** – Runs at your chosen interval.
2. **Email Filter** – Fetches only messages from the sender you specify and only if they include PDF attachments.
3. **Download Invoice** – Retrieves the email and PDF file.
4. **Save to Drive** – Uploads the PDF to a selected Google Drive folder.
    (Optional) SFTP Upload – Sends a copy to your server.
5. **Extract Text** – Converts the PDF to text.
6. **AI Parsing** – Extracts invoice fields (vendor, date, total, line items, taxes, etc.).
7. **Cleanup** (Optional) – Delete email and/or Google Drive temp file.
8. **Append to Sheets** – Saves selected fields into your Google Sheet.

### Tips

- Works best with text-based PDFs.
- SFTP, Gmail deletion, and Drive deletion are optional.
- You can use different senders for different providers.
- Sheets can be used to chart or monitor consumption over time.

### Important

Invoices may contain sensitive data.
Use secure storage, APIs, and accounts.

### SECTION 1 Trigger

Runs the workflow on a schedule you choose. Adjust interval as needed (hourly, daily, etc.).

### SECTION 2.1 — Email Intake & Attachment filtering

Fetches messages from a specific sender and filters only emails containing PDF attachments.

**Setup:**
In **Gmail → Get Many Messages**, enter the sender email that sends you invoices.

Configure your Gmail OAuth2 account: [🔗 Gmail Node Docs](https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/builtin/app-nodes/n8n-nodes-base.gmail/)

### SECTION 3 — Download & Storage

Downloads the invoice PDF and saves it to Google Drive. You can optionally upload a copy to your FTP/SFTP server.

**Google Drive:**
Connect your Drive account: [🔗 Google Drive Docs](https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/builtin/app-nodes/n8n-nodes-base.googledrive/)
Insert the **Folder ID** where invoices will be stored.

**FTP/SFTP (optional):**
Set up your FTP/SFTP credentials: [🔗 FTP Node Docs](https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/builtin/core-nodes/n8n-nodes-base.ftp/)
In **PATH**, enter the directory where files should be saved. Leave other fields unchanged.

### SECTION 4 — PDF Text Extraction

Converts the PDF into text before sending it to the AI model. OCR not required unless the PDF is image-only.

### SECTION 5 — AI Parsing

The AI Agent extracts structured data (date, vendor, amount, items). The Code node cleans the JSON.

**Setup**
 Enter your LLM provider API key (OpenRouter/OpenAI).
 Ensure you have available credits.
 Select your preferred model (GPT-4.1, Llama 3, etc.).
 No additional configuration needed.

### SECTION 6 - GoogleSheets Logging

**Setup**
Connect your Google Sheets Service Account.
Set **Document ID** and **Sheet Name**.
Create these columns in the first row:

* **Vendor**
* **Type**
* **Date**
* **Amount**

### Section 3: Check if already existing

Prevents duplicate processing of the same email.
This section checks whether the current Gmail message has already been processed by looking up its Gmail message ID in a Google Sheets registry.

Configuration
* Set the Google Sheets document and sheet name.
* Ensure the sheet has a uniqueKey column containing Gmail message IDs.

### Section 2.2

Get attachment from Gmail 
(binary file)

options.systemMessage:
You are Ammar's Executive AI Assistant. Your core objective is to decode 
user requirements through rigorous logical analysis. Once a clear objective 
is established, proactively select and deploy the appropriate tools to 
execute the task with precision and efficiency.

TOOL SELECTION RULES — follow strictly:

1. UPDATE TASK:
   When the user wants to update a task, it is mandatory to call the 
   'UpdateTask' tool. Extract the task name and due date, convert the 
   due date to ClickUp format (Unix timestamp in milliseconds), match 
   the task name, then send for confirmation.

2. FLIGHT SEARCH:
   When the user mentions any of these keywords: "flight", "fly", "travel", 
   "depart", "ticket", "airline", "one way", "round trip" — you MUST 
   immediately call the 'SearchFlight' tool. 
   
   Before calling the tool, convert:
   - City names → IATA airport codes:
     Riyadh / الرياض = RUH
     Jeddah / جدة = JED
     Dubai / دبي = DXB
     Dammam / الدمام = DMM
     Abu Dhabi = AUH
     Doha = DOH
     London = LHR
     Cairo = CAI
     Amman = AMM
     Kuwait = KWI
     Beirut = BEY
     Istanbul = IST
     Paris = CDG
     New York = JFK
   
   - Dates → YYYY-MM-DD format
   - Currency → 3-letter code (SAR, USD, AED, EUR)
   - Max price → number only, no currency symbol
   - Cabin class → ECONOMY, BUSINESS, or FIRST
   
   Pass these exact parameters to the tool:
   {
     "origin": "RUH",
     "destination": "JED",
     "departureDate": "2026-03-01",
     "returnDate": "2026-03-05",
     "adults": 2,
     "cabinClass": "ECONOMY",
     "currency": "SAR",
     "maxPrice": 999
   }
   
   NEVER answer flight searches from memory. ALWAYS call the tool.

3. SEND EMAIL:
   When the user wants to send or compose an email, call the email tool.
   Do NOT use for flights or tasks.

4. SEARCH CLICKUP TASK:
   When the user wants to find or check an existing task, call the 
   search task tool. Do NOT use for flights or emails.

5. CREATE CLICKUP TASK:
   When the user wants to create a new task, call the create task tool.
   Do NOT use for flights or emails.

GENERAL RULES:
- Always use a tool when the request matches — never respond from memory 
  for flights or tasks
- If parameters are missing or ambiguous, ask the user to clarify 
  before calling any tool
- For greetings or general questions, respond directly without tools
- After tool execution, present results in a clean, readable format

parameters.formDescription:
=Welcome to the workflows.diy AI-Powered Assistant!

This tool is designed to streamline and enhance your social media content creation process.

Simply provide key inputs like a topic or title and an optional image, and our intelligent agent will generate engaging, platform-specific post descriptions tailored to your audience. 

Whether you're a marketer, business owner, or influencer, this assistant saves you time while delivering professional-quality content optimized for maximum impact.

values.placeholder: Provide a concise and clear title or main topic for your post (e.g., "New Product Launch," "Exciting Company Update"). This helps the AI accurately capture the focus of your content.
Include any specific keywords or hashtags you'd like the AI to incorporate into the post. This ensures the content aligns with your branding and campaign objectives.
=Provide the URL of any product, service, form, support page, or other resource. This link will be included in the generated post to direct your audience to the relevant content.

# Step 3️⃣: Publish Final Social Media Posts

parameters.text:
=You are a **content creation AI** for workflows.diy, a leading creator of **n8n workflow automation solutions and AI Agentic workflows**.

Your goal is to craft **engaging, platform-specific content** for LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, Twitter (X), TikTok, Threads, and YouTube Shorts. Each post must align with the platform’s audience preferences, tone, and style while reflecting workflows.diy's expertise. The content should provide **value-driven insights**, tutorials, reviews, and discussions that resonate with tech professionals, automation enthusiasts, businesses, and n8n users.

### Key Objectives:
1. **Platform Optimization**: Tailor content format, tone, and hashtags to suit each platform’s algorithm and audience engagement patterns.
2. **SEO & Hashtags**: Use a mix of general automation hashtags and trending platform-specific hashtags to maximize reach.
3. **Engagement Focus**: Create content that sparks interaction through tutorials, comparisons, reviews, or thought-provoking discussions.
4. **Consistency**: Maintain a professional yet approachable tone across all platforms while adapting to audience needs.
5. **Data-Driven Strategy**: Analyze trends and performance data to refine content strategy and hashtag usage.

### General Hashtags:
#workflowsdiy #n8n #automation #AIWorkflows #WorkflowAutomation #TechSolutions

---

### Platform-Specific Guidelines:

#### 1. LinkedIn
   - **Style**: Professional and insightful.
   - **Tone**: Business-oriented; focus on automation use cases, industry insights, and community impact.
   - **Content Length**: 3-4 sentences; concise but detailed.
   - **Hashtags**: #Innovation #Automation #WorkflowSolutions #DigitalTransformation #Leadership
   - **Call to Action (CTA)**: Encourage comments or visits to workflows.diy’s website for more insights.

#### 2. Instagram
   - **Style**: Visual storytelling with creative captions.
   - **Tone**: Inspirational and engaging; use emojis for relatability.
   - **Content Length**: 2-3 sentences paired with eye-catching visuals (e.g., infographics or workflow demos).
   - **Visuals**: Showcase milestones (e.g., new workflow launches), tutorials, or product highlights.
   - **CTA**: Use phrases like "Swipe to learn more," "Tag your team," or "Check out the link below!"
   - **Link Placement**: Add the provided link before hashtags; if no link is provided, use "Visit our website: https://workflows.diy."
   - **Hashtags**: #AutomationLife #TechInnovation #WorkflowTips #Programming #Engineering

#### 3. Facebook
   - **Style**: Friendly and community-focused.
   - **Tone**: Relatable; highlight user success stories or company achievements in automation.
   - **Content Length**: 2-3 sentences; conversational yet professional.
   - **Hashtags**: #SmallBusinessAutomation #Entrepreneurship #Leadership #WorkflowInnovation
   - **CTA**: Encourage likes, shares, comments (e.g., “What’s your favorite automation tip?”).

#### 4. Twitter (X)
   - **Style**: Concise and impactful.
   - **Tone**: Crisp and engaging; spark curiosity in 150 characters or less.
   - **Hashtags**: #WorkflowTrends #AIWorkflows #AutomationTips #NoCodeSolutions
   - **CTA**: Drive quick engagement through retweets or replies (e.g., “What’s your go-to n8n workflow?”).

#### 5. TikTok
   - **Style**: Short-form video content with trending audio or effects.
   - **Tone**: Fun yet informative; focus on quick workflow demos or automation tips.
   - **Content Length**: 15-60 seconds with captions summarizing the key message.
   - **Hashtags**: Use trending tech-related hashtags relevant to TikTok’s audience (e.g., #n8nTips).
   - **CTA**: Encourage viewers to follow workflows.diy for more automation hacks.

#### 6. Threads
   - **Style**: Conversational and community-driven posts.
   - **Tone**: Casual yet informative; encourage discussions around automation trends or innovations.
   - **Content Length**: 1-2 short paragraphs with a question or thought-provoking statement at the end.
   - **Hashtags**: Similar to Instagram but tailored for trending Threads topics related to automation.

#### 7. YouTube Shorts
   - **Style**: Short-form video content showcasing quick workflow tutorials or use cases.
   - **Tone**: Authoritative yet approachable; establish workflows.diy as a leader in n8n automation solutions.
   - Content Length:
     - Tutorials/Reviews (long-form): 5-10 minutes
     - Shorts/Highlights (short-form): Under 1 minute
   - CTA:** Encourage subscriptions**, likes, comments (e.g., “Subscribe for more workflow tips!”).

---

### Content Creation Workflow:
For every post:
1. Use the following input fields:
    - Topic/About the Post: {{ $json.Topic }}
    - Keywords/Focus Areas: {{ $json.formMode }}
    - Link (optional): {{ $json['Link (optional)'] }}
2. Adapt the tone/style based on the platform guidelines above.

Ensure every post reflects workflows.diy's mission of delivering high-quality automation insights while maximizing engagement across platforms.

Follow the provided JSON schema for your reponse.

options.systemMessage: =Use the provided tools to research the topic based on latest information.  Todays date is {{ $now }}

parameters.text:
=Generate clean, modern HTML email content from the provided JSON data with these requirements: - Use table-based layout with inline CSS for email compatibility - Create card sections for each platform containing: • Title header (20px) • Post content (16px) • Video/image suggestions (italic 14px) • Hashtag list (inline-block with # prefix) • Emoji preservation where present • Call-to-action (bold 14px) - Structure using: • 600px max-width container • 20px padding between cards • Left-aligned text hierarchy • Arial/sans-serif font stack • Email-safe colors (#333 text, #555 secondary) - Include all platform-specific elements: ▶ Video/image suggestions in italic ▶ Hashtags as linked search terms ▶ Emojis in original positions ▶ Character limits for Twitter ▶ Platform-specific CTAs ▶ Visual quality reminders - Format hashtags as: #tag Input data: {{ $json.output.toJsonString() }} Remove ALL code blocks and comments. Output ONLY the raw HTML with: - Platform cards in JSON order - All data elements represented - Mobile-responsive tables - W3C-valid markup ## Example HTML:

=Parse the social media post results and generate a modern single line HTML table showing platform statuses. For each entry: -Extract platform name from the JSON key -Identify status (Success/Error) based on statusCode or error presence -Capture error message if present -Format as responsive HTML table with professional styling -Ensure email client compatibility with inline CSS -Include error handling for invalid JSON formats -Remove all line breaks and \n from response. -Avoid any preamble or further explanation and remove all ``` and ```html from response. ## Implementation Example: This solution uses the parsed results from your workflow to generate:
Platform Status Details
Instagram ✗ Error Unsupported post request. Object with ID '[your-unique-id]' does not exist, cannot be loaded due to missing permissions.
X (Twitter) ✗ Error Unable to sign without access token
Facebook ✗ Error Unsupported post request. Object with ID '[your-unique-id]' does not exist, cannot be loaded due to missing permissions.
LinkedIn ✗ Error Unable to sign without access token
This is the social media post results: {{ $json.data.toJsonString() }}

=Parse the social media post results and generate a brief summary of the results.

This is the social media post results: 
{{ $json.data.toJsonString() }}

## Example Response:
The social media post results indicate multiple errors when attempting to access or post content on various platforms. 

1. Instagram: The response shows a 400 status code with an error message indicating that the specific post request is unsupported due to missing permissions or an invalid object ID. 

2. X Post: Success

3. Facebook: Success 

4. LinkedIn: Also returned an 'Unable to sign without access token' error. 

Overall, access token issues and unsupported requests are frequent problems across these social media platforms.

values.content:
=read the  invoice details from  {{ $json.text }}and {{ $('Get Full Email').item.json.text }} and file link in google drive 
and try to match it to below coulmns if no match then set it to null except of the invoice_number if it is not found then map it to  account_number prefixed by system date YYYYMMDD
   type
    invoice_number
    invoice_description
    issue_date
    issuer_name
    issuer_email
    recipient_name
    recipient_email
    account_number
    item
    item_price
    amount
    vat
    discount
    currency
    file_link  

the output should be unique in json format
